## [00:00 - 03:00]

Now another area that I like to hack into, and I've actually found some decent strategies to test as well, is Facebook's success stories website. So the link is below and you can go and have a look at it. And it's updated, I think every week, like we're part of the Facebook Agency Partner Program, we get updates on all the latest case studies that come out, but they're all here for you to use. If you go into this section, you can see all of the case studies that have been submitted into Facebook. And it gives you an idea as to what other people are doing. And you can break it down by the type of business, what kind of objectives they're running, their industry products, for example. So if we go into e-commerce, and let's say we wanna find an example in e-commerce, someone that's using, let's say for example, someone that's using collection ads. I'm a big fan of collection ads, for example. So we come into here, let's say we want, I mean, we'll just have a look. We've got e-com collection ads. I'm not gonna go further into region or anything like that. So we can have a quick look around, see if anything catches our eyes. Gantt, Gantt Norway, let's have a look at that. So we have a look at this, and it gives you an idea of the case study. So there is a particular format that when you submit a case study to Facebook, they ask for specific things pivoted around the products. That's one thing that Facebook wants to focus on is case studies that use different products. So if you are looking to submit a case study, focus on what products that you're using and how that ties into your case study. So let's have a look at this case study that just pulled up randomly. All American clothing brand, they're in advertising in Norway, and the success story is based on 2.5x increase in conversions, 2.3x increase in revenue, and a 16x return on ad spend, not bad at all. And it gives you an idea of the brand and what their goal was in increase revenue and how they achieved it. So it talks here about their actual strategy. So here's the thing, I see so many people relying on Facebook groups, LinkedIn groups, Twitter, et cetera to hack into all the latest things that people are doing and find new strategies and tactics and things like that. This is actually one of my better sources of inspiration where I frequent this probably weekly, maybe every other week to find new and interesting case studies where I can create ideas for my own testing from. I'll give you an example. Back in 2018, when we were playing around with CBO, Campaign Budget Optimization, the thing that cracked it for me was finding a Facebook success story where a particular brand, I think it was in South America, had used CBO, but they switched their daily,

## [03:00 - 06:02]

that their ad set optimization from seven-day, click one day view to one-day, click one day view. And I took that away and I thought, okay, that's interesting. They got some good results, I'm gonna test that. I tested it and it worked. For the first time, I got CBO to work. This is, I think, Q2 kind of early to mid 2018. And for the first time, I got results from CBO. And then I went deeper into it. And from that process, the next three to four months, we carried on testing and refining and really figured out how to make the CBO system work. And I wonder, like, if I didn't read that case study, would I have just thought to tweak seven-day to one-day to make it work? Now, it's not to say that is a solution for everyone, it just worked for us at that particular time. But that led us down a different path and that came from a Facebook case study. of spend on ads, resulting in zero conversions by 76%. So they've actually optimized their spend based on using automation.
